data.title: Tesla to install semi chargers at Pilot travel centers along major trucking routes
data.description:
• Tesla (TSLA) has inked a deal with Pilot Travel Centers to install heavy-duty electric truck charging stations at certain Pilot stations along major trucking routes in California, Georgia, Nevada, New Mexico, and Texas, where the need for charging is highest.
  • “Heavy-duty charging is yet another extension of our exploration into alternative fuel offerings, and we’re happy to partner with a leader in the space that provides turnkey solutions and deploys them quickly,” said Shannon Sturgil, senior vice president of alternative fuels at Pilot.
  • The charging stations will initially only accommodate Tesla (TSLA) semis but may be expanded in the future to be compatible with other heavy-duty EVs.
  • Construction on the charging network will begin in the first half of 2026 and is expected to be ready for use by this summer.
  • With more than 900 travel centers in 44 U.S. states and six Canadian provinces, Pilot has the largest network of travel centers in North America.

data.title: Cloudflare soars as AI chatbot running on its infrastructure goes viral
data.description:
Cloudflare (NET) shares had jumped 12% by noon trading on Tuesday, following reports of an AI chatbot running on Cloudflare's infrastructure that had gone viral.

This followed Cloudflare's 10% run-up during Monday trading.

The AI assistant, originally named Clawdbot, was renamed to Moltbot on Tuesday after Anthropic (ANTHRO) asked the bot's creator, Peter Steinberger, to change the name due to potential trademark issues, according to Forbes.

Moltbot is an open-source AI agent built on Anthropic's Claude models. Its connection to Cloudflare stems from the fact that as these agents scale, they require secure and low-latency infrastructure. Cloudflare's global edge network provides this necessary support.

"We believe Cloudflare's price surge is being caused by increasing awareness of Cloudflare's role in AI infrastructure due to social media discussions related to an open-source AI assistant built on Anthropic's Claude model, Clawdbot," said TD Cowen analyst Shaul Eyal in an investor note.

"The focus of the interest is Cloudflare Tunnel, which allows developers to connect locally run tools such as AI agents on the internet without exposing their servers," Eyal added.

Moltbot functions as a digital helper. It can perform tasks such as managing a user's inbox and calendar, drafting messages, checking travelers in for flights, and writing code, all with minimal prodding.

It can run within a wide array of applications, such as Meta's (META) WhatsApp, Discord (DSCD), Salesforce's (CRM) Slack, Microsoft (MSFT) Teams, Apple's (AAPL) iMessage, Spotify (SPOT), Canvas, OpenAI's (OPENAI) ChatGPT, xAI's (X.AI) Grok, and dozens more.

data.title: Lam Research leans on AI tailwinds as China weighs on outlook
data.description:
Lam Research (LRCX) is scheduled to announce Q2 earnings results on Wednesday, January 28th, after market close.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $1.17 and the consensus revenue estimate is $5.24B.

Over the last 2 years, LRCX has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 100% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 2 upward revisions and 1 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 2 upward revisions and 1 downward.

On ratings, Lam Research holds a Buy consensus on Wall Street, with an average price target of $215.35, implying about 9.8% downside. In contrast, the Seeking Alpha Quant model rates the stock as Hold, citing a stretched valuation. LRCX's stock momentum is strong, with a one-year price performance of 215.3%, far above the sector median.

In October, Lam Research (LRCX) posted strong Q1 results, with revenue up 27.6% year over year to $5.32B and EPS of $1.26, beating expectations on both revenue and earnings.

While this performance remains a positive driver, management signaled slower growth ahead. For Q2, Lam guided revenue to about $5.2B ± $300M, implying roughly 21% year-over-year growth at the midpoint, down from 27% in Q1. The primary cause for this slowdown is cited to be the Chinese end market.

Bullish view

“Lam Research benefits from a razor-and-blades model, generating high-margin, recurring service revenue that mitigates cyclicality and enhances profitability. Major tailwinds include surging AI compute demand, rising process intensity, advanced packaging adoption, and memory chip shortages driving capacity expansion,” SA analyst Cedric Pfort said.

Cautious view

“The recently implemented 50% affiliate rule as well as the correction of the overordering from the Chinese market in 2023-2024 should pose a considerable headwind in the coming few quarters in the company's revenue, especially given its substantial (30-40% of total revenue) revenue contribution,” explains SA analyst Alpha Mantra. As per management, they expect ~$200M and $600M revenue impact from these headwinds in 2QFY26 and full-year FY26, respectively.

data.title: Alcoa downgraded at Morgan Stanley after strong YTD performance
data.description:
Alcoa (AA) +1.6% in Tuesday's trading despite receiving a downgrade to Equal Weight from Overweight with a $64 price target at Morgan Stanley, which sees a more balanced risk-reward after the stock significantly outperformed peers in recent weeks, up nearly 50% since December 1.

Alcoa (AA) is taking several actions to increase productivity, reduce costs and optimize its asset portfolio, and the company will be receiving IRA production tax credits for U.S. aluminum production of $50M-$60M/year, Morgan Stanley analyst Carlos De Alba said.

A tighter aluminum market should keep profitability elevated while U.S. regional premiums are now reflecting a 50% tariff, and the company has identified 10 high-priority curtailed sites that it is actively marketing to hyperscalers for data center conversion; however, the stock's strong YTD outperformance leaves the stock with a more balanced risk-reward, the analyst said.

While Alcoa (AA) should continue to benefit from elevated London Metal Exchange aluminum pricing and a U.S. Midwest permium that recently broke the $1/lb level, these factors are now properly reflected in the company's share price, according to De Alba.

Also, the timeline for a potential transaction to transform a curtailed site into a data center continues to get pushed back, and the recent escalation in trade tensions between the U.S. and Canada suggests a potential Section 232 exemption for Canadian aluminum may be more difficult, the analyst said.

data.title: Cedar Creek Partners raises stake in PHI Group during Q4 2025
data.description:
• Cedar Creek Partners, which focuses primarily on microcap stocks, and over-the-counter stocks in particular, returned 2.4% in the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • "Most of the fund's performance in the fourth quarter was due to the increase in the valuation of our control position. It increased 14% in the quarter. Our expert market stocks increased in value by just under 3% in the fourth quarter, while our generally undervalued securities increased less than 1%," said the fund letter.
  • During the quarter, the fund increased its position in PHI Group (PHIG) and added a new major expert market position in EXCO Resources (EXCE).
  • Source: Fund Letter

data.title: RTX outlines 2026 targets with $92–$93B sales and $8.25–$8.75B free cash flow driven by strong backlog
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: RTX Corporation (RTX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Christopher Calio, Chairman & CEO, stated RTX delivered "strong sales, adjusted EPS and free cash flow in the fourth quarter, underscoring our momentum and focus on execution across RTX." He highlighted full year adjusted sales of $88.6 billion, adjusted EPS of $6.29, and free cash flow of $7.9 billion. Calio emphasized a record backlog of $268 billion, up 23% year-over-year, with $161 billion of commercial orders and $107 billion of defense awards, and noted "orders and commitments during the year included over 1,500 GTF engines and over 2,400 Pratt Canada engines."
  • On defense, Calio detailed major awards such as $1.2 billion to supply Spain with Patriot air and missile defense systems, and a $1.2 billion contract for Tamir missile production. He noted Raytheon's international backlog mix now stands at 47%.
  • Calio explained, "For 2026, we expect adjusted sales to be between $92 billion and $93 billion, with 5% to 6% organic growth year-over-year." He guided for adjusted EPS between $6.60 and $6.80 and free cash flow of $8.25 billion to $8.75 billion.
  • Calio detailed investments, stating "in 2025, we invested over $10 billion in CapEx and company and customer-funded research and development," and projected $10.5 billion in 2026, including $3.1 billion in CapEx.
  • Neil Mitchill, Executive VP & CFO, stated, "In the fourth quarter, adjusted sales of $24.2 billion were up 12% on an adjusted basis and 14% organically." He added, "Adjusted segment operating profit of $2.9 billion was up 9% year-over-year, and adjusted earnings per share of $1.55 was up 1% from prior year."

Outlook

  • The company projects adjusted sales for 2026 of $92 billion to $93 billion, representing 5% to 6% organic growth. Adjusted EPS is expected to be between $6.60 and $6.80, with free cash flow between $8.25 billion and $8.75 billion.
  • Mitchill described, "By sales channel at the RTX level and adjusting for divestitures, we expect both commercial OE and defense to grow mid-single digits and commercial aftermarket to be up high single digits."
  • Segment guidance includes Collins sales up mid-single digits adjusted and high single digits organically, with operating profit growth of $425 million to $525 million. Pratt & Whitney is expected to see sales up mid-single digits and operating profit growth of $225 million to $325 million. Raytheon is projected for mid- to high single-digit sales growth and operating profit up $200 million to $300 million.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted sales for Q4 were $24.2 billion, up 12% adjusted and 14% organically. Adjusted segment operating profit was $2.9 billion, with adjusted EPS at $1.55. GAAP EPS from continuing operations was $1.19. Free cash flow for the quarter was $3.2 billion, including approximately $1 billion of powder metal-related compensation and $600 million of tariff impacts.
  • Collins Q4 sales were $7.7 billion, up 3% adjusted and 8% organically. Pratt & Whitney Q4 sales reached $9.5 billion, up 25% on both an adjusted and organic basis. Raytheon Q4 sales were $7.7 billion, up 7% adjusted and organic.
  • For the year, Collins generated $30.2 billion in adjusted sales, Pratt $32.9 billion, and Raytheon $28 billion.

Q&A

  • Peter Arment, Baird, asked for an update on the GTF fleet management plan. Calio responded, "Our financial technical outlook remain on track and consistent with our prior comments. AOGs did come down in Q4, and they're down over 20% from the highs of 2025."
  • Ronald Epstein, BofA Securities, inquired about handling capital allocation amid government scrutiny. Calio said, "We remain committed to the dividend. That said, again, we're comfortable we can accommodate both that and the investment needs that come with delivering the current backlog."
  • Kristine Liwag, Morgan Stanley, asked about portfolio composition amid industry changes. Calio replied, "RTX is constructed to meet the moment... our breadth and scale provides a competitive advantage... We're going to continue to invest, and we've got the balance sheet to continue to invest in the capacity and in technology."
  • Robert Stallard, Vertical Research, questioned Pratt & Whitney's OEM forecast. Mitchill said, "Large commercial engine output in terms of deliveries is likely to be up call it, mid- to high single digits."
  • Scott Deuschle, Deutsche Bank, asked about Pratt commercial aftermarket growth. Mitchill explained, "About 70% of Pratt's growth in '26 is going to come from commercial aftermarket... V2500 shop visits... probably within 20 shop visits of 2025, so steady and above 800."
  • Myles Walton, Wolfe Research, inquired about Raytheon segment growth drivers. Mitchill responded, "The majority of that sales increase is coming from the Land and Air Defense Systems business... about 85% of our '26 sales are sitting in our backlog today."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment, focusing on execution, capital allocation, and growth opportunities, with questions signaling confidence but seeking clarity on segment-specific details and strategic priorities.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, emphasizing execution, backlog strength, and investment capability. Calio used phrases like "we have great momentum heading into 2026" and "fully supportive of the transformation initiatives."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remains positive. Management's confidence is sustained, with ongoing emphasis on operational execution and demand trends.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full year 2026 is now provided, with sales, EPS, and free cash flow targets higher than the prior quarter's full year 2025 guidance. Margin expansion, backlog growth, and segment outlooks indicate strengthened confidence.
  • Strategic focus continues on operational execution, capacity expansion, and digital solutions. The backlog rose from $251 billion at Q3 to $268 billion at Q4. Segment-level margin improvement and investment in capacity are more pronounced.
  • Analysts' focus has shifted to longer-term portfolio strategy, supply chain health, and managing increased demand.
  • Management's tone is consistently confident, with emphasis on execution and meeting both current and projected demand.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing supply chain challenges and the need for increased production capacity, particularly for munitions and critical components.
  • Tariff impacts, powder metal-related compensation, and pension-related items continue to be headwinds, though mitigation plans are in place. Mitchill referenced "$1 billion of powder metal-related compensation and approximately $600 million of tariff-related impacts" in Q4.
  • Analysts highlighted risks around balancing capital allocation, supply chain constraints, and the ability to ramp production to meet backlog demands. Management reaffirmed commitment to mitigating these issues through investment and collaboration with government partners.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized RTX's strong close to 2025 and an optimistic outlook for 2026, driven by record backlog, robust demand across commercial and defense segments, and significant planned investments in capacity and technology. The company remains focused on operational execution, cost discipline, and delivering on backlog while managing industry challenges and capital allocation priorities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: CME metals volume hits new single-day record as gold, silver prices rally
data.description:
CME Group's (CME) metals complex hit a single-day record of 3.34M contracts on Jan. 26, up 18% from the prior daily record of 2.83M contracts on Oct. 17, 2025, the derivatives exchange said on Tuesday.

The move coincides with gold's (XAUUSD:CUR) surge to over $5K per ounce and silver's (XAGUSD:CUR) jump to more than $100 per ounce, as macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainties continued to drive demand for traditional safe havens.

"Amid ongoing macro-economic uncertainty, record volatility and heightened price risk, clients are turning to our markets to hedge and adjust precious metals exposure to meet their trading goals," said Jin Hennig, managing director and Global Head of Metals at CME Group.

Micro silver futures helped drive the record trading day in metals, posting a daily record volume of 715.1K contracts and record open interest of 35.7K contracts. It was also a top five trading day for silver futures, micro gold futures and one-ounce gold futures.

data.title: Holiday movies, key games boost viewership for Disney, Warner Bros. in December — Nielsen
data.description:
Nielsen's "Media Distributor Gauge" report for December 2025 showed that platforms airing key sports events and movies saw “record-breaking” viewership during the holiday season.

The report, which is an aggregated view of total TV usage by media company, showed that Disney (DIS) captured the highest multiplatform increase, helped by a 30% viewership increase on ESPN, which was driven by Monday Night Football, the expanded College Football Playoffs schedule, and College Gameday.

The programming of holiday films like Rudolph The Red-Nosed Reindeer and Frosty The Snowman on Freeform also helped Disney in December.

Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) benefitted during December through its cable networks, TBS and TNT, whose viewership climbed 23% and 24%, respectively. 

"These gains were driven by College Football playoff coverage and holiday movie viewership, with A Christmas Story leading the way for TBS," Nielsen said. "HBO Max also saw a 10% viewing increase, primarily driven by original series It: Welcome To Derry, alongside the continued popularity of The Big Bang Theory and Friends."

Nielsen noted that Amazon (AMZN) saw viewership rise 12% in December, attributable to Thursday Night Football coverage, Christmas Day NFL game, and the new season of Fallout.

The Hallmark Channel benefitted the most from holiday movie programming in December, Nielsen said.

The network maintained strong momentum from November and secured the top five movie telecasts across cable distributors, namely She’s Making A List, Single On The 25th, A Suite Holiday Romance, A Make Or Break Holiday, and Oy To The World.

By distributor rankings, the Nielsen report indicated that YouTube (GOOGL) (GOOG) was in the lead in December and had a 12.7% total share of viewership, followed by Disney with 10.7% and Netflix (NFLX) with 9%.

 

 

Earlier this month, Nielsen's “Gauge” report, which is the leading media sector snapshot of total TV and streaming consumption, showed that streaming viewership captured 47.5% of television in December 2025, while broadcast and cable had a share of 21.4% and 20.2%, respectively.

 

 

Pay TV distributors: Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA), Charter (NASDAQ:CHTR), Dish Network (NASDAQ:SATS), Verizon FiOS (NYSE:VZ), Optimum/Suddenlink (NYSE:ATUS), Atlantic Broadband (OTCPK:CGEAF), Sparklight (NYSE:CABO).

Relevant local broadcast tickers: Nexstar Media Group (NASDAQ:NXST), Sinclair Broadcast Group (NASDAQ:SBGI), Gray Television (NYSE:GTN), Tegna (NYSE:TGNA), E.W. Scripps (NASDAQ:SSP).

National broadcasters: ABC (NYSE:DIS), NBC (NASDAQ:CMCSA), CBS (NASDAQ:PSKY), Fox (NASDAQ:FOX) (NASDAQ:FOXA).

Ad-tech names tied to connected TV: The Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD), Magnite (NASDAQ:MGNI), PubMatic (NASDAQ:PUBM), Criteo (NASDAQ:CRTO), Roku (NASDAQ:ROKU), Illumin (OTCQB:ILLMF).

data.title: SentinelOne gains after bullish pitch from Citron Research
data.description:
SentinelOne (S) rose 4% after a bullish post from Citron Research.

"SentinelOne is time to pound the table," Citron wrote in a posting on X on Tuesday.  "Clawdbot is the tell- Autonomous Agents, the conversation has just begun, and $S has the lead in protecting the house." 

SentinelOne (S) shares have dropped 37% over the past year. 

This isn't the first time Citron has pitched SentinelOne (S). Last month, Citron said the company remains undervalued following its transition from an endpoint security vendor to an AI-native data and security platform. At the time, Citron set a $32 price target on the stock.
